questions.question: The story 'His First Flight' is written by _______.
questions.explanation: Liam O'Flaherty was an Irish novelist and short story writer who wrote 'His First Flight'.

questions.question: The theme of the story is _______.
questions.explanation: The story's central theme is overcoming fear, as the young seagull conquers his fear of flying.

questions.question: The young seagull was afraid of _______.
questions.explanation: The young seagull was terrified of taking his first flight from the high ledge.

questions.question: How many brothers did the young seagull have?
questions.explanation: The text states 'His two brothers and his sister had already flown away.'

questions.question: The young seagull also had a _______.
questions.explanation: The text mentions 'his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own.'

questions.question: The seagull's siblings' wings were _______ compared to his.
questions.explanation: The text says 'his brothers and his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own.'

questions.question: 'Ledge' means _______.
options: a narrow surface on a cliff
questions.correct_answer: a narrow surface on a cliff
questions.explanation: A ledge is defined as 'a narrow surface that continues out from the side of a cliff, wall, or other surface.'

questions.question: 'Expanse' means _______.
options: a large area of land, water, or sky
questions.correct_answer: a large area of land, water, or sky
questions.explanation: Expanse is defined as 'a large area of land, water, or sky' in the textbook glossary.

questions.question: 'Muster up' means _______.
options: to make an effort to produce a feeling
questions.correct_answer: to make an effort to produce a feeling
questions.explanation: Muster up means 'make an effort to produce a feeling or reaction from within oneself.'

questions.question: The young seagull's parents threatened to _______.
questions.explanation: His parents 'threatened to let him starve on his ledge unless he flew away.'

questions.question: How long had nobody come near the young seagull?
questions.explanation: The text states 'That was twenty-four hours ago. Since then nobody had come near him.'

questions.question: The seagull's older brother caught his first _______.
questions.explanation: The text says 'he had seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it.'

questions.question: 'Devour' means _______.
questions.explanation: Devour means 'to eat something very fast' according to the textbook glossary.

questions.question: 'Cackle' means _______.
questions.explanation: Cackle is defined as '(of a bird) a loud, unpleasant, clucking cry.'

questions.question: The family laughed at the young seagull's _______.
questions.explanation: The text says the family had been 'laughing at his cowardice.'

questions.question: The only food the seagull found on his ledge was _______.
options: a dried piece of mackerel tail
questions.correct_answer: a dried piece of mackerel tail
questions.explanation: The text says 'he had found a dried piece of mackerel tail at the far end of his ledge.'

questions.question: The young seagull even gnawed at _______.
questions.explanation: He 'even gnawed at the dried pieces of eggshell. It was like eating part of himself.'

questions.question: 'Daintily' means _______.
options: in a delicate and graceful manner
questions.correct_answer: in a delicate and graceful manner
questions.explanation: Daintily is defined as 'in a delicate and graceful manner.'

questions.question: 'Precipice' means _______.
questions.explanation: Precipice is defined as 'a very steep high cliff.'

questions.question: To catch his parents' attention, the seagull pretended to _______.
questions.explanation: He 'closed one eye, then the other, and pretended to be falling asleep.'

questions.question: 'Preening' means _______.
questions.explanation: Preening is defined as 'cleaning and arranging feathers with the beak or tongue.'

questions.question: Who was the only one looking at the young seagull?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Only his mother was looking at him.'

questions.question: The mother seagull was tearing at a piece of _______.
questions.explanation: The text says 'she tore at a piece of fish that lay at her feet.'

questions.question: 'Whet' in the story means _______.
questions.explanation: Whet means '(here) make the beak sharper.'

questions.question: The young seagull cried _______ begging for food.
questions.explanation: The text says ''Ga, ga, ga,' he cried, begging her to bring him over some food.'

questions.question: 'Mockingly' means _______.
options: in an unkind or rude manner
questions.correct_answer: in an unkind or rude manner
questions.explanation: Mockingly is defined as 'in an unkind or rude manner.'

questions.question: 'Plaintively' means _______.
questions.explanation: Plaintively is defined as 'in a sad manner.'

questions.question: 'Abreast' means _______.
options: facing or moving in the same direction
questions.correct_answer: facing or moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: Abreast means 'facing or moving in the same direction.'

questions.question: What made the young seagull finally dive off the ledge?
options: hunger drove him to dive at the fish
questions.correct_answer: hunger drove him to dive at the fish
questions.explanation: 'Maddened by hunger, he dived at the fish' — his desperate hunger made him dive.

questions.question: When the seagull first fell, he felt _______.
questions.explanation: 'A monstrous terror seized him and his heart stood still.'

questions.question: The young seagull realised he was _______.
questions.explanation: 'He was soaring gradually downwards and outwards. He was no longer afraid.'

questions.question: 'Commenced' means _______.
questions.explanation: Commenced is defined as 'began.'

questions.question: 'Ridges' on the sea means _______.
options: long narrow raised lines on the surface
questions.correct_answer: long narrow raised lines on the surface
questions.explanation: Ridges means 'long narrow raised lines along the surface of something' — here, the waves.

questions.question: When the seagull's legs sank into the sea, he _______.
questions.explanation: 'He was floating on it' — after his feet and belly touched the water, he floated.

questions.question: The family offered the young seagull scraps of _______.
questions.explanation: The text says 'their beaks were offering him scraps of dog-fish.'

questions.question: Liam O'Flaherty was born in _______.
questions.explanation: The textbook states 'Liam O'Flaherty (1896–1984).'

questions.question: Liam O'Flaherty was a _______ writer.
questions.explanation: The textbook describes him as 'an Irish novelist and short story writer.'

questions.question: Two constant themes in O'Flaherty's works are _______.
questions.explanation: 'Two constant themes in his works are nature and Ireland.'

questions.question: The mother's cry 'Gawl-ool-ah' was said _______.
questions.explanation: The text says ''Gawl-ool-ah,' she screamed back mockingly.'

questions.question: The mother halted with the fish _______ the young seagull's beak.
questions.explanation: The text says 'the piece of fish in her beak almost within reach of his beak.'

questions.question: The seagull's ledge faced _______.
questions.explanation: The text says 'his ledge that faced south.'

questions.question: The seagull's father was _______ on his white back.
questions.explanation: 'His father was preening the feathers on his white back.'

questions.question: The young seagull tried to reach his parents without _______.
questions.explanation: He walked back and forth 'trying to find some means of reaching his parents without having to fly.'

questions.question: After his first flight, the young seagull's family was _______.
questions.explanation: 'Around him his family was screaming, praising him, and their beaks were offering him scraps of dog-fish.'

questions.question: The last line of the story is _______.
questions.explanation: The story ends with the triumphant line: 'He had made his first flight.'

questions.question: 'Skim the waves' means _______.
questions.explanation: Skim the waves means 'almost touching the waves.'

questions.question: The young seagull's nest was made of _______.
questions.explanation: The text mentions 'the rough, dirt-caked straw nest where he and his brothers and sister had been hatched.'

questions.question: The siblings and parents were on a plateau _______ the opposite cliff.
questions.explanation: 'The big plateau midway down the opposite cliff.'

questions.question: Between the young seagull and his parents there was _______.
questions.explanation: 'Between him and his parents there was a deep, wide crack.'

questions.question: The young seagull uttered a _______ scream when he first flew.
questions.explanation: 'He uttered a joyous scream and flapped them again. He soared higher.'
